stories.text: As an application developer, I want to subscribe to a channel with a predicate (WHERE amount > 1000 AND region = 'eu') so that my consumer only receives messages matching my criteria without filtering in application code.
As an application developer, I want to compose predicates with AND, OR, and NOT so that I can express complex routing rules declaratively without writing a routing function.
As an application developer, I want to deploy a custom routing function in Gleam so that I can implement load-aware or state-dependent routing logic that predicates cannot express.
As an application developer, I want to hot-deploy an updated routing function so that I can change routing logic in production without restarting the bus or dropping connections.
As an application developer, I want to configure a pressure policy on my channel so that the bus automatically handles overload without my consumers needing custom backpressure code.
As an application developer, I want to receive typed backpressure signals (accept, defer, reject) on every publish so that my producer can adapt its rate based on real-time consumer capacity.
As an AI agent, I want to declare my max in-flight message count at subscription time so that the bus never sends me more concurrent work than I can handle.
As an AI agent, I want to receive a defer signal when the bus buffers a message for me so that I know my capacity limits are being respected and work is queued, not dropped.
As an AI agent, I want to join a consumer group and receive work via dispatch conversations so that I share load with other agents without manual coordination or partition assignment.
As an AI agent, I want my crash during message processing to automatically re-route the message to another agent so that no work is lost due to my failure.
As the Aion engine, I want to dispatch activity tasks to workers via channel routing so that worker selection uses the bus's load-aware routing instead of round-robin gRPC dispatch.
As the Aion engine, I want dispatch conversations to detect worker crashes via process links so that failed activities are re-routed within milliseconds, not heartbeat intervals.
As the Aion engine, I want to add or remove worker consumers without triggering a rebalance so that scaling workers does not pause in-progress activities.
As an operator, I want to observe per-channel pressure metrics (in-flight count, buffer depth, reject rate) so that I can identify overloaded channels before they degrade service quality.
As an operator, I want to see which pressure policy actions have triggered and when so that I can verify the bus is responding to overload as configured.
As an operator, I want to receive scale-consumer signals from the bus so that I can trigger auto-scaling in my orchestrator when consumer capacity is exhausted.
